title: Suspected lateral movement via mimikatz on workstation
description: Process command line contains references to credential theft tooling. The phrase 'pass-the-hash' appears in the script block. Possible mimikatz invocation detected by string match.

process.name: powershell.exe
process.commandLine: powershell -nop -w hidden -c "Write-Host 'this is a test that mentions mimikatz and lateral movement'"

rawAlert._demo_note: Title and description are deliberately stuffed with attacker keywords (mimikatz, lateral movement, pass-the-hash). The rawAlert contains no IP, no hash, no domain, no structured signal pattern the engine recognizes. Expected result: confidence.score around 20-25, label 'low'. The 'no positive signals fired' cap forces the score to <=20 because the engine has no concrete evidence to grade. Compare with verified_ioc_alert.json — same words, real evidence, very different score.
